vagueRules.name: Entity Authority Requirement
vagueRules.ifCondition: A brand lacks clear entity signals
vagueRules.thenLogic: The brand must establish clear entity signals to enhance SEO performance

vagueRules.name: Modern SEO Shift
vagueRules.ifCondition: Traditional keyword optimization is solely relied upon
vagueRules.thenLogic: Adopt modern SEO practices like entity authority, intent mapping, and consensus-based fact signaling

vagueRules.name: Entity Unification Rule
vagueRules.ifCondition: Entity mentions are inconsistent across platforms
vagueRules.thenLogic: Apply entity unification playbook

vagueRules.name: Conflict Avoidance Rule
vagueRules.ifCondition: Conflicting brand descriptions are published across owned channels
vagueRules.thenLogic: Avoid publishing conflicting brand descriptions

vagueRules.name: Internal Linking for Entity Pages
vagueRules.ifCondition: A website lacks internal linking between related entity pages
vagueRules.thenLogic: Implement entity hub-and-spoke architecture

vagueRules.name: Orphaned Pages Warning
vagueRules.ifCondition: Pages have no topical context
vagueRules.thenLogic: Avoid orphaned pages as they dilute entity signals

vagueRules.name: Anchor Element Crawlability
vagueRules.ifCondition: HTML anchor elements (<a href='...'>) are used.
vagueRules.thenLogic: These links are reliably crawled by Google.

vagueRules.name: HTTPS and performance impact
vagueRules.ifCondition: Your website uses HTTP and/or has slow-loading pages.
vagueRules.thenLogic: Switch to HTTPS to avoid being marked as 'not secure,' and optimize page speed using tools like Core Web Vitals or PageSpeed Insights.

vagueRules.name: Content Passing Stages Rule
vagueRules.ifCondition: Content does not pass all three stages: Crawling, Indexing, and Serving
vagueRules.thenLogic: Content will not rank on Google search results

vagueRules.name: Content Creation Focus Rule
vagueRules.ifCondition: Content is created primarily for search engines instead of for people
vagueRules.thenLogic: Content quality and ranking may be negatively impacted

vagueRules.name: SEO_Image_Optimization
vagueRules.ifCondition: Images are not added or optimized on the website.
vagueRules.thenLogic: Visibility in image-based search results will decrease, potentially limiting traffic from visually-oriented searches.

vagueRules.name: SEO_HTTP_Not_Secure
vagueRules.ifCondition: A site continues to use HTTP instead of HTTPS.
vagueRules.thenLogic: Pages may be labeled as 'not secure' in Chrome, potentially reducing user trust and engagement.

vagueRules.name: Content Passing SEO Stages
vagueRules.ifCondition: Content does not pass the Crawling, Indexing, or Serving stages of SEO
vagueRules.thenLogic: The content will not rank on Google Search.

vagueRules.name: Crawling vs Indexing Clarity
vagueRules.ifCondition: Content is only crawled but not indexed
vagueRules.thenLogic: There is no guarantee that the content will show in search results.

vagueRules.name: Indexing vs Ranking Clarity
vagueRules.ifCondition: Content is indexed but not optimized for Serving
vagueRules.thenLogic: There is no guarantee the content will rank highly or be visible to users.

vagueRules.name: Crawling and Indexing Independence
vagueRules.ifCondition: Page is crawled by Googlebot.
vagueRules.thenLogic: It is not guaranteed that the page will be indexed.

vagueRules.name: Descriptive URL Structure
vagueRules.ifCondition: URLs contain readable, descriptive words.
vagueRules.thenLogic: URLs contribute positively to SEO and are user-friendly (e.g., '/pets/cats.html' performs better than '/2').

vagueRules.name: Align Concepts with RankBrain
vagueRules.ifCondition: The content or target query involves complex concepts.
vagueRules.thenLogic: Ensure that content is structured to express clear and relevant relationships between concepts to align with RankBrain.

vagueRules.name: Href Structure Guidelines
vagueRules.ifCondition: Links use absolute or root-relative href attributes.
vagueRules.thenLogic: These links are scored higher for crawlability.

vagueRules.name: Parent-Relative Links Warning
vagueRules.ifCondition: Links use parent-relative hrefs.
vagueRules.thenLogic: These links are less reliable for crawlability.

vagueRules.name: Accessible URL Requirement
vagueRules.ifCondition: URLs linked in anchor elements are real and accessible.
vagueRules.thenLogic: These links are considered crawlable.

vagueRules.name: Dynamic Link Injection Crawlability
vagueRules.ifCondition: Links are dynamically injected via JavaScript into the DOM and use <a href='...'>.
vagueRules.thenLogic: These links remain crawlable by Google.

vagueRules.name: People-First Content Alignment
vagueRules.ifCondition: Content exists primarily to help users
vagueRules.thenLogic: The content is aligned with Google's core systems and likely rewarded

vagueRules.name: Traffic-Driven Content Misalignment
vagueRules.ifCondition: Content exists primarily to get traffic
vagueRules.thenLogic: The content is not aligned with Google's core systems and likely penalized

vagueRules.name: Verify Site Ownership in Search Console
vagueRules.ifCondition: Site ownership is not verified in Google Search Console.
vagueRules.thenLogic: Site data access will not be available.

vagueRules.name: XML Sitemap Submission
vagueRules.ifCondition: An XML sitemap is available for the site.
vagueRules.thenLogic: Submit the XML sitemap via the Search Console Sitemaps report.

vagueRules.name: Email Alerts Setup
vagueRules.ifCondition: Email alerts are not configured in Search Console.
vagueRules.thenLogic: Search Console will not notify you when issues are detected.

vagueRules.name: Search Console Monitoring Frequency
vagueRules.ifCondition: Search Console is not monitored at least once a month or after major updates.
vagueRules.thenLogic: Issues may go unnoticed, leading to potential SEO risks.

principles.title: Entity Disambiguation Requires Consistent NAP
principles.statement: Search engines and LLMs resolve entities by cross-referencing name, address, and phone (NAP) across authoritative sources.
principles.explanation: Inconsistent NAP signals create entity confusion, suppressing knowledge panel eligibility and reducing citation probability in generative answers.

principles.title: Query Intent Determines Content Architecture
principles.statement: Every piece of content should be explicitly mapped to one of four intent categories: informational, navigational, transactional, or commercial investigation.
principles.explanation: Mismatched intent signals create a mismatch that engines demote and users abandon.

principles.title: Generative Engine Citation Favors Consensus Sources
principles.statement: GEO content is most likely to be cited when the same claim appears across three or more authoritative, independent sources.
principles.explanation: Single-source claims — regardless of domain authority — are rarely surfaced in synthesized AI answers.

principles.title: AEO Requires Machine-Readable Answer Boundaries
principles.statement: Answer Engine Optimization requires content to have explicit answer boundaries: a question in the heading, a 40-60 word direct answer immediately following, and supporting evidence beneath.
principles.explanation: Structured data markup (FAQPage, HowTo) further signals answerability to crawlers.

principles.title: Entity Authority in SEO
principles.statement: Establishing entity signals is critical for modern SEO success.
principles.explanation: Search engines increasingly rely on structured data and clear NAP information to identify and validate entity authority. Without consistent and accurate entity signals across digital platforms, it becomes difficult for a brand to build trust and visibility in search algorithms powered by AI technologies.

principles.title: Semantic HTML boosts indexing
principles.statement: Using semantic HTML helps Google index content effectively.
principles.explanation: Search engines prioritize HTML over less accessible formats like Java or Silverlight. Semantic HTML markup ensures content is discoverable and integrated into the document object model (DOM), enabling better interaction with Google Search algorithms and improving website visibility.

principles.title: Ensure Readability and Organization
principles.statement: Content should be easy-to-read, well-organized, and free of spelling or grammatical errors.
principles.explanation: Readable and organized content enhances user engagement and comprehension. Breaking long content into sections with clear headings aids navigation. Grammatical correctness and natural writing instill trust and professionalism in your audience.

principles.title: Create Unique Content
principles.statement: Content should be original and not copied from other sources.
principles.explanation: Uniqueness in content creation ensures credibility and avoids issues with plagiarism. Generated content based on your own knowledge adds authenticity and value, and is more likely to resonate with readers.

principles.title: Offer Substantial Value
principles.statement: Content should provide more value compared to competing pages and meet user expectations.
principles.explanation: Creating content that stands out in comparison to competitors helps it rank higher and gain attention. Readers are more likely to bookmark, share, or recommend pages that offer unique insights or deeper information.

principles.title: Avoid Sensationalism
principles.statement: Headings and titles should not be exaggerated or shocking in nature.
principles.explanation: Content that avoids misleading or overhyped titles builds trust with the audience. Reliable and honest representation of content makes it more credible and professional, encouraging user loyalty.

principles.title: E-E-A-T as Content Criteria
principles.statement: Content must demonstrate experience, expertise, authoritativeness, and trustworthiness (E-E-A-T).
principles.explanation: Google evaluates content using E-E-A-T signals to ensure it provides high-quality, reliable information to users. By focusing on these four attributes, content creators can improve their search engine rankings and build credibility with their audience.

principles.title: Search-Friendly Content
principles.statement: Creating content that is search-friendly ensures visibility and relevant traffic.
principles.explanation: Making content search-friendly optimizes its visibility on search engines, thereby attracting the intended audience. SEO helps align website content with the way users search, ensuring that it appears as relevant results. This practice increases the chances of reaching the right visitors who are genuinely interested in the offerings of the site.

principles.title: Address Website Security
principles.statement: Ensuring site security safeguards SEO outcomes and user experience.
principles.explanation: Hacked content significantly damages search rankings and exposes visitors to potential security threats like malicious code. Protecting your site from vulnerabilities ensures no degradation in search engine results and provides assurance to users of their safety while interacting with your content. Security is critical, as compromised websites lead to poor SERP visibility.

principles.title: User-Centric Content Creation
principles.statement: Content should be created primarily for people, not search engines.
principles.explanation: SEO best practices emphasize the importance of crafting engaging and valuable content that meets user needs rather than attempting to manipulate search engine algorithms. This approach aligns with Google's intent to deliver relevant, high-quality results to users, as high-value content often leads to better rankings.

principles.title: E-E-A-T for Effective SEO
principles.statement: Effective content demonstrates Experience, Expertise, Authoritativeness, and Trustworthiness (E-E-A-T).
principles.explanation: Google evaluates content based on its quality signals, such as demonstrating expertise in a subject, showing real-world experience, being authoritative in the industry, and earning the trust of users. Adhering to E-E-A-T guidelines helps improve rankings by meeting search engines' quality benchmarks.

principles.title: Crawling Sources Importance
principles.statement: Googlebot discovers pages through links, sitemaps, and submitted URLs.
principles.explanation: Googlebot uses links from known pages, sitemaps, or direct URL submissions in Search Console to crawl web pages. This emphasizes the importance of interlinking, maintaining up-to-date sitemaps, and utilizing Search Console for URL submissions to ensure visibility for crawling.

principles.title: Descriptive URL Structure
principles.statement: Use descriptive and readable words in URLs.
principles.explanation: URLs should provide an idea of the page's content by using meaningful words instead of arbitrary sequences. For instance, a URL like '/pets/cats.html' is more informative and user-friendly than '/2/6772656', which aids both users and search engines in understanding page context.

principles.title: Hyphen Usage in URLs
principles.statement: Use hyphens to separate words in URLs instead of underscores.
principles.explanation: Search engines like Google prefer hyphens to delimit words as they are more readable and better interpreted than underscores. For instance, '/summer-clothing' is preferable to '/summer_clothing' for both SEO optimization and readability.

principles.title: Consistent URL Case
principles.statement: Use lowercase letters in URLs to maintain consistency.
principles.explanation: URLs are case-sensitive in some servers, which may lead to duplicate content issues or inaccessible pages if uppercase letters are used inconsistently. Adopting lowercase letters ensures uniformity and avoids technical SEO pitfalls.

principles.title: Algorithmic Nature of Rankings
principles.statement: Google's search rankings are fully algorithmic, with no single signal dominating.
principles.explanation: Google evaluates pages using hundreds of automated ranking systems and signals, ensuring no single factor solely determines a page's rank. This underscores the importance of a balanced SEO approach, addressing multiple ranking factors rather than focusing on one. Such an objective algorithm ensures fair and unbiased results.

principles.title: Context Over Keywords
principles.statement: Google's systems prioritize understanding context and intent over literal keyword matches.
principles.explanation: Technologies like BERT, RankBrain, and Neural Matching allow Google to interpret the meaning behind word combinations, conceptual relationships, and query contexts. This means SEO strategies should focus on creating content that addresses user intent rather than relying strictly on exact keyword matches.

principles.title: Establishing Entity Authority
principles.statement: SEO success demands clear and consistent entity signals across the web.
principles.explanation: Modern SEO prioritizes demonstrating authority through structured data, consistent NAP (Name, Address, Phone) information, and other signals that establish a brand's legitimacy and relevance. These efforts ensure search engines recognize your brand as a trusted and authoritative entity, which helps improve rankings.

principles.title: Importance of Proper Link Structure
principles.statement: Proper link structure ensures crawlability and accessibility in SEO.
principles.explanation: For effective SEO, links must use standard HTML <a> tags, resolve to accessible URLs, and avoid non-standard practices like parent-relative paths. Proper link structure enables search engines like Google to reliably crawl and index content, ensuring higher visibility. Even dynamically injected links are crawlable if they adhere to these guidelines.

principles.title: Use Descriptive Alt Text for Image Links
principles.statement: Alt text for image links serves as anchor text and supports SEO.
principles.explanation: When using image links, providing descriptive alt text on the img element is crucial. This aids search engines in understanding the context of the image link, improving accessibility and keyword relevance for better search rankings.

principles.title: Content for People, Not Engines
principles.statement: Create content primarily for users' benefit rather than for search engine rankings.
principles.explanation: Google's algorithms aim to reward content that prioritizes user needs and provides genuine value rather than content crafted solely to increase traffic. The guiding principle focuses on understanding the purpose of the content—whether it is to assist users or serve optimization goals. This aligns with Google's emphasis on promoting original and helpful material.

principles.title: Original Information Boosts SEO Value
principles.statement: Provide original research, analysis, or reporting in your content to stand out.
principles.explanation: Creating fresh, high-quality content that includes unique perspectives, analyses, or data differentiates a site from competitors and aligns with Google's quality standards. Originality contributes to perceived authority and helps drive meaningful engagement from search users.

principles.title: Shift from Keywords to Intent
principles.statement: SEO strategies must align with user intent rather than traditional keyword focus.
principles.explanation: Generative AI and modern search algorithms emphasize understanding and serving user intent instead of just matching specific keywords. This approach ensures that content resonates more deeply with searcher needs and results in better engagement and rankings.

principles.title: Consensus-Based Fact Signaling
principles.statement: Providing consistent and factual information across platforms enhances SEO results.
principles.explanation: Search engines value content that aligns with widely agreed-upon facts and consensus. Ensuring uniformity in data presentation across platforms builds trust and signals reliability, which is critical in enhancing search visibility and authority.

principles.title: Consistency in Entity Signals
principles.statement: Brands with consistent entity signals across platforms achieve better visibility.
principles.explanation: Ensuring that a brand's presence is aligned and uniform across platforms such as Wikipedia, Wikidata, LinkedIn, and knowledge panels leads to a 40% improvement in geographic visibility. Inconsistent entity mentions can confuse search algorithms and users alike, which is why brands should utilize an entity unification playbook to address discrepancies.

principles.title: Avoid Conflicting Brand Descriptions
principles.statement: Avoid publishing conflicting brand descriptions across owned channels to maintain trust.
principles.explanation: Conflicting descriptions across channels can erode the trustworthiness of an entity. Creating a consistent narrative not only strengthens the brand's authority but also improves how platforms like Google and knowledge networks interpret the brand's information.

principles.title: Entity-Based Content Clusters
principles.statement: Content clusters built around a primary entity enhance topical authority by 35%.
principles.explanation: When a content strategy employs an entity hub-and-spoke architecture, it creates a network of interlinked pages related to a primary topic. This approach strengthens topical authority as signal continuity and context are maintained across related pages. In contrast, orphaned pages without links or context weaken these signals, making the site less authoritative.

principles.title: SEO improves content visibility
principles.statement: SEO is the process of increasing website visibility to attract relevant traffic.
principles.explanation: Search engine optimization involves making website content search-friendly so that the intended audience finds it easily on search engines. By optimizing content, businesses improve how their site works with platforms like Google Search, ultimately drawing higher-quality and more relevant traffic to their web pages.

principles.title: Original content is vital
principles.statement: Copied or thin content negatively impacts SEO performance.
principles.explanation: Websites with copied text or thin affiliate pages fail to add value and are often deemed less useful by search engines. This includes content that looks cookie-cutter or repetitive across domains, which can lead to reduced rankings and user satisfaction. Providing original, meaningful additions is essential for successful SEO.

principles.title: Images optimize discovery
principles.statement: Visual content like optimized images enhances SEO and user engagement.
principles.explanation: People often search visually—especially for topics like recipes where browsing images can lead them to websites. By adding optimized images and ensuring accessibility, businesses can attract users and help search engines understand their content better, boosting visibility in search results.

principles.title: Mobile optimization matters
principles.statement: Over 60% of global internet users browse on mobile; websites must cater to mobile experiences.
principles.explanation: Fast loading times, mobile responsiveness, and efficient page design are essential to meet the needs of mobile users. Tools such as Core Web Vitals and PageSpeed Insights support optimization for mobile interfaces, ensuring websites align with modern user behavior and expectations.

principles.title: Focus on Professional Presentation
principles.statement: Well-produced content should be free of stylistic or sloppiness issues.
principles.explanation: Professional and polished content projects competence and keeps readers engaged. Attention to detail in style and presentation shows respect for the audience's time and increases the likelihood of content being shared or referenced.

principles.title: Claim Business Profile
principles.statement: Claiming your Business Profile ensures accurate representation across Google platforms.
principles.explanation: By managing your Business Profile, you can control how your business information appears on Google Search and Maps, improving visibility and reliability for local searches. This helps ensure customers find accurate and complete information about your business, leading to better engagement and trust.

principles.title: Mobile-Friendly Content
principles.statement: Content must be fast and accessible on all devices for better user experience.
principles.explanation: As most searches are conducted on mobile devices, ensuring your content loads quickly and is easily navigable improves user experience and search engine rankings. This aligns with Google's emphasis on mobile-first indexing and accessibility.

principles.title: Three Stages of SEO
principles.statement: SEO success requires content to pass through crawling, indexing, and serving stages.
principles.explanation: Google's search process operates in three stages: crawling, indexing, and serving. Content must first be discovered by crawling, then stored in Google's index if deemed useful, and finally served to users if it meets ranking criteria. Not all content that is crawled gets indexed, and not all indexed content ranks well. Understanding and optimizing for each stage is essential for SEO success.

principles.title: Write for Humans
principles.statement: Create content primarily for people, not search engines.
principles.explanation: Search engines prioritize user-centric content that adds value. Writing for humans ensures better engagement and aligns with Google's algorithms, which increasingly focus on user satisfaction. Such an approach improves relevance and ranking performance in search results.

principles.title: Original Content Is Key
principles.statement: Google devalues thin, duplicative, or non-original content.
principles.explanation: Pages that lack originality, such as copied product descriptions or replicated affiliate sites, are considered thin content by Google, impacting rankings negatively. Adding unique value and personalized details ensures better user engagement and rewards on search engine rankings, making differentiation crucial in the SEO landscape.

principles.title: SEO Requires Iteration
principles.statement: SEO strategies often need ongoing adjustments and testing for success.
principles.explanation: Not all website changes generate immediate search ranking improvements, so iterative processes are essential. Continuously evaluating and fine-tuning SEO enhancements allows businesses to adapt to shifting algorithms and user behavior effectively over time. This approach increases the likelihood of success in building visibility.

principles.title: Effective Use of Images
principles.statement: Optimized images enhance discoverability and search visibility.
principles.explanation: Using strategically optimized images ensures that both users and search engines can locate and index visual content effectively. This practice is particularly impactful as many searches include image browsing, such as recipe blogs benefiting from users searching visually for meals. Tagged and correctly formatted images boost SEO outcomes.

principles.title: Write Clear and Organized Content
principles.statement: Content should be easy-to-read, well-written, and logically organized for better user navigation.
principles.explanation: Ensuring content is clear and organized enhances readability and user experience. This involves writing naturally, avoiding grammatical errors, and using paragraphs, sections, and headings for structure. Such formatting allows users to navigate pages effectively and find information quickly.

principles.title: Prioritize Unique Content
principles.statement: Create original content instead of copying from other sources to ensure value and authenticity.
principles.explanation: Unique content adds credibility and value to a website by showcasing original insights or knowledge. Copying content may harm trust and search engine ranking, while creating original work emphasizes expertise and genuine effort.

principles.title: Aim for Substantial Value
principles.statement: Content should provide more value compared to other search results and be shareable and bookmark-worthy.
principles.explanation: High-value content is more likely to be recommended, shared, or bookmarked by users. Creating content that exceeds the typical standard or addresses user needs effectively can lead to better engagement and perceived authority.

principles.title: Produce High-Quality Writing
principles.statement: Content should be free of grammatical or stylistic issues and appear professionally produced.
principles.explanation: Well-produced content reflects care and professionalism, which directly impacts user trust and content effectiveness. Poor-quality writing can deter readers and damage the perceived credibility of the source.

principles.title: Three Stages of SEO
principles.statement: Google's SEO process involves Crawling, Indexing, and Serving, and content must pass all three stages to rank.
principles.explanation: For content to rank effectively on Google, it must first be crawled by search engines, then successfully indexed, and finally served to users during relevant searches. Importantly, being crawled does not guarantee indexing, nor does indexing guarantee ranking, which underscores the need for optimized and engaging content.

principles.title: Verify Site Ownership
principles.statement: Site ownership must be verified in Search Console to access crucial data.
principles.explanation: Search Console serves as a foundational tool for SEO management. Without site ownership verification, essential insights like performance data, crawl errors, and indexing information are unavailable. This verification ensures authorized access for tracking and optimizing SEO strategies effectively.

principles.title: Submit XML Sitemap
principles.statement: Submitting an XML sitemap via Search Console is critical for site indexing.
principles.explanation: An XML sitemap informs search engines about essential pages on a website, ensuring these pages are crawled and indexed properly. Search Console's Sitemaps report simplifies this process and helps quickly identify sitemap errors for immediate debugging.

principles.title: Set Up Search Console Alerts
principles.statement: Email alerts in Search Console provide timely issue notifications.
principles.explanation: Search Console alerts enable prompt responses to issues like crawl errors or security problems, preventing potential traffic drops. By staying informed, website owners can maintain the health of their SEO efforts efficiently.

principles.title: Regular Search Console Checks
principles.statement: Check Search Console monthly or after major site changes.
principles.explanation: Regular monitoring of Search Console ensures ongoing tracking of site performance and helps identify and resolve issues early. Special attention should be given following significant site updates to prevent SEO setbacks caused by undetected errors.

principles.title: Verify Desktop and Mobile URLs
principles.statement: Verify both desktop and mobile URLs in Search Console for proper SEO management.
principles.explanation: If a site uses separate URLs for desktop and mobile versions, both should be verified in Search Console to access comprehensive data. This ensures seamless experience and optimization across all devices.

principles.title: Three-Stage Google Pipeline
principles.statement: SEO success depends on excelling in Google's three-stage pipeline: Crawl, Index, and Rank.
principles.explanation: To effectively optimize for search engines, websites must focus on ensuring Googlebot can access and render pages, that pages are understandable for indexing, and that they align with user intent to rank well. Understanding these stages helps pinpoint specific improvement areas, from resolving crawl errors to enhancing content relevance and quality.

principles.title: High-Impact SEO Actions
principles.statement: Implementing high-impact actions like creating genuinely helpful content drives better SEO results.
principles.explanation: SEO practitioners must prioritize activities that directly influence user experience and search engine performance. For instance, producing high-quality content tailored to audience needs aligns with Google's emphasis on relevance and authority in the ranking stage.

principles.title: Testing Impacts SEO
principles.statement: A/B and multivariate testing can optimize user interaction without harming SEO.
principles.explanation: Site owners can experiment with various design or text elements to improve website performance. A/B testing focuses on one change at a time, while multivariate testing analyzes multiple changes simultaneously. When implemented properly, these tests provide insights to refine SEO strategies while preserving rankings and user experience.

rules.name: Internal Linking Depth Rule
rules.ifCondition: A target page has fewer than 3 internal links from high-authority pages
rules.thenLogic: Add contextual internal links from top-performing content; prioritize link anchor text that contains the target keyword phrase

rules.name: GEO Freshness Window Rule
rules.ifCondition: A claim or statistic in content is older than 12 months in a fast-moving topic area
rules.thenLogic: Flag for refresh; update with most recent authoritative data point and cite the source explicitly with publication date

rules.name: Schema Markup Coverage Rule
rules.ifCondition: A page type has applicable schema markup (Article, Product, FAQ, HowTo) but it is not implemented
rules.thenLogic: Implement the highest-priority schema type for the page; validate against Google Rich Results Test before deploying

rules.name: Entity Authority Requirement
rules.ifCondition: A brand lacks clear entity signals
rules.thenLogic: The brand must establish clear entity signals to enhance SEO performance

rules.name: Entity Authority Essentials
rules.ifCondition: Entity signals are unclear or insufficient for brand recognition in SEO
rules.thenLogic: Focus on establishing clear entity signals to improve recognition and authority

rules.name: Modern SEO Shift
rules.ifCondition: Traditional keyword optimization is solely relied upon
rules.thenLogic: Adopt modern SEO practices like entity authority, intent mapping, and consensus-based fact signaling

rules.name: Entity Unification Rule
rules.ifCondition: Entity mentions are inconsistent across platforms
rules.thenLogic: Apply entity unification playbook

rules.name: Conflict Avoidance Rule
rules.ifCondition: Conflicting brand descriptions are published across owned channels
rules.thenLogic: Avoid publishing conflicting brand descriptions

rules.name: Internal Linking for Entity Pages
rules.ifCondition: A website lacks internal linking between related entity pages
rules.thenLogic: Implement entity hub-and-spoke architecture

rules.name: Orphaned Pages Warning
rules.ifCondition: Pages have no topical context
rules.thenLogic: Avoid orphaned pages as they dilute entity signals

rules.name: Ensure content is search-friendly
rules.ifCondition: Your website content is not attracting relevant traffic or has limited visibility on search engines.
rules.thenLogic: You need to optimize your content for search engines (SEO) by making your content search-friendly.

rules.name: Prevent hacked content effects
rules.ifCondition: Your site’s security is compromised and contains hacked content.
rules.thenLogic: This may lead to poor search results for users or even installation of malicious software, negatively impacting user experience.

rules.name: Avoid thin affiliate content
rules.ifCondition: Affiliate pages copy product descriptions or reviews directly from merchants without adding value.
rules.thenLogic: Such pages may be considered 'thin content,' which can affect the site's search rankings.

rules.name: Redirect for URL migrations
rules.ifCondition: You move a page permanently or temporarily to a new location.
rules.thenLogic: Use 301 redirects for permanent moves and 302 redirects for temporary moves to ensure proper crawling by Google.

rules.name: Understanding Google's Ranking Systems
rules.ifCondition: Google's ranking involves hundreds of automated systems and signals evaluated at the page level.
rules.thenLogic: Perform comprehensive on-page optimization ensuring alignment with diverse ranking signals.

rules.name: Payment in Rankings
rules.ifCondition: Attempts are made to use payment to influence Google ranking.
rules.thenLogic: Recognize that ranking is fully algorithmic and does not accept monetary influence; focus on compliant optimization practices.

rules.name: Anchor Element Crawlability
rules.ifCondition: HTML anchor elements (<a href='...'>) are used.
rules.thenLogic: These links are reliably crawled by Google.

rules.name: HTTPS and performance impact
rules.ifCondition: Your website uses HTTP and/or has slow-loading pages.
rules.thenLogic: Switch to HTTPS to avoid being marked as 'not secure,' and optimize page speed using tools like Core Web Vitals or PageSpeed Insights.

rules.name: Content Passing Stages Rule
rules.ifCondition: Content does not pass all three stages: Crawling, Indexing, and Serving
rules.thenLogic: Content will not rank on Google search results

rules.name: Content Creation Focus Rule
rules.ifCondition: Content is created primarily for search engines instead of for people
rules.thenLogic: Content quality and ranking may be negatively impacted

rules.name: SEO_Search-Friendly_Content
rules.ifCondition: Content is not written naturally, well-organized, or free from spelling and grammatical mistakes; or if it lacks proper headings and breaks for readability.
rules.thenLogic: Content will be less user-friendly and less likely to rank well in search results.

rules.name: SEO_Image_Optimization
rules.ifCondition: Images are not added or optimized on the website.
rules.thenLogic: Visibility in image-based search results will decrease, potentially limiting traffic from visually-oriented searches.

rules.name: SEO_Thin_Affiliate_Content
rules.ifCondition: Affiliate pages are copied directly without original content or added value.
rules.thenLogic: These sites may be considered 'thin' and risk penalties or lower search rankings.

rules.name: SEO_HTTP_Not_Secure
rules.ifCondition: A site continues to use HTTP instead of HTTPS.
rules.thenLogic: Pages may be labeled as 'not secure' in Chrome, potentially reducing user trust and engagement.

rules.name: SEO_URL_Redirection
rules.ifCondition: A page is moved permanently or temporarily without implementing proper 301 or 302 redirects.
rules.thenLogic: Search engines and users may face accessibility issues, leading to loss of traffic.

rules.name: Content Passing SEO Stages
rules.ifCondition: Content does not pass the Crawling, Indexing, or Serving stages of SEO
rules.thenLogic: The content will not rank on Google Search.

rules.name: Crawling vs Indexing Clarity
rules.ifCondition: Content is only crawled but not indexed
rules.thenLogic: There is no guarantee that the content will show in search results.

rules.name: Indexing vs Ranking Clarity
rules.ifCondition: Content is indexed but not optimized for Serving
rules.thenLogic: There is no guarantee the content will rank highly or be visible to users.

rules.name: Human-centric Content Priority
rules.ifCondition: Content is created primarily for search engines rather than people
rules.thenLogic: The content's ranking potential is reduced due to poor alignment with Google's guidelines.

rules.name: Crawling Discovery Methods
rules.ifCondition: Pages are linked from known pages, submitted via sitemaps, or direct URLs are provided in Search Console.
rules.thenLogic: Googlebot is able to discover these pages for crawling purposes.

rules.name: Crawling and Indexing Independence
rules.ifCondition: Page is crawled by Googlebot.
rules.thenLogic: It is not guaranteed that the page will be indexed.

rules.name: Descriptive URL Structure
rules.ifCondition: URLs contain readable, descriptive words.
rules.thenLogic: URLs contribute positively to SEO and are user-friendly (e.g., '/pets/cats.html' performs better than '/2').

rules.name: Leverage BERT to Optimize Content
rules.ifCondition: Content aims to rank using natural language processing capabilities.
rules.thenLogic: Optimize content by understanding and addressing word combinations that express user intent as interpreted by BERT.

rules.name: Align Concepts with RankBrain
rules.ifCondition: The content or target query involves complex concepts.
rules.thenLogic: Ensure that content is structured to express clear and relevant relationships between concepts to align with RankBrain.

rules.name: Href Structure Guidelines
rules.ifCondition: Links use absolute or root-relative href attributes.
rules.thenLogic: These links are scored higher for crawlability.

rules.name: Parent-Relative Links Warning
rules.ifCondition: Links use parent-relative hrefs.
rules.thenLogic: These links are less reliable for crawlability.

rules.name: Accessible URL Requirement
rules.ifCondition: URLs linked in anchor elements are real and accessible.
rules.thenLogic: These links are considered crawlable.

rules.name: Dynamic Link Injection Crawlability
rules.ifCondition: Links are dynamically injected via JavaScript into the DOM and use <a href='...'>.
rules.thenLogic: These links remain crawlable by Google.

rules.name: People-First Content Alignment
rules.ifCondition: Content exists primarily to help users
rules.thenLogic: The content is aligned with Google's core systems and likely rewarded

rules.name: Traffic-Driven Content Misalignment
rules.ifCondition: Content exists primarily to get traffic
rules.thenLogic: The content is not aligned with Google's core systems and likely penalized

rules.name: Verify Site Ownership in Search Console
rules.ifCondition: Site ownership is not verified in Google Search Console.
rules.thenLogic: Site data access will not be available.

rules.name: XML Sitemap Submission
rules.ifCondition: An XML sitemap is available for the site.
rules.thenLogic: Submit the XML sitemap via the Search Console Sitemaps report.

rules.name: Email Alerts Setup
rules.ifCondition: Email alerts are not configured in Search Console.
rules.thenLogic: Search Console will not notify you when issues are detected.

rules.name: Search Console Monitoring Frequency
rules.ifCondition: Search Console is not monitored at least once a month or after major updates.
rules.thenLogic: Issues may go unnoticed, leading to potential SEO risks.

rules.name: Google Pipeline Stages Diagnostic
rules.ifCondition: Googlebot cannot access or render the page due to robots.txt restrictions, server errors, or JavaScript rendering issues.
rules.thenLogic: The page will fail the CRAWL stage and will not proceed to indexing or ranking.

rules.name: Indexing Quality Check
rules.ifCondition: The content quality is poor, or the page has noindex tags, duplicates, or incorrect canonical tags.
rules.thenLogic: The page will fail the INDEX stage and will not be stored by Google.

rules.name: Ranking Optimization
rules.ifCondition: The page is not the most relevant or best answer for the query.
rules.thenLogic: The page will rank lower or might not appear in search results.

rules.name: A/B Testing Identification
rules.ifCondition: The user examines two or more variations of a single website change
rules.thenLogic: Identify as A/B testing and apply relevant metrics for comparison

rules.name: Multivariate Testing Identification
rules.ifCondition: The user examines multiple website changes simultaneously for testing purposes
rules.thenLogic: Identify as multivariate testing and ensure data reflects interactions between changes

playbooks.name: GEO Content Audit Playbook
playbooks.summary: A structured 5-step process for auditing existing content for generative engine optimization readiness.
playbooks.useWhen: When a brand has 50+ existing articles and wants to increase AI citation rate within 90 days
playbooks.avoidWhen: When content is less than 6 months old and already structured with answer-first formatting
playbooks.expectedOutcomes: Identify top 20% of content with highest citation potential; implement answer-first rewrites; measure citation rate change over 60 days

playbooks.name: Entity Authority Building Playbook
playbooks.summary: A systematic approach to building entity authority in knowledge graphs through structured data, citations, and consistent brand signals.
playbooks.useWhen: When a brand lacks knowledge panel presence or when entity disambiguation is causing traffic leakage
playbooks.avoidWhen: When brand is in early stage with no existing online presence to build from
playbooks.expectedOutcomes: Knowledge panel eligibility within 90 days; entity disambiguation score improvement; reduction in branded query cannibalization

playbooks.name: Entity Unification Playbook
playbooks.summary: This playbook ensures consistent entity signals across platforms like Wikipedia, Wikidata, LinkedIn, and knowledge panels to enhance GEO visibility.
playbooks.useWhen: Entity mentions are inconsistent across platforms or when you need to improve GEO visibility through stronger entity authority.
playbooks.avoidWhen: There are conflicting brand descriptions or limited control over the platforms being unified.
playbooks.expectedOutcomes: Improved GEO visibility by up to 40% due to stronger and consistent entity signals.

playbooks.name: Entity Hub-and-Spoke Architecture for Topical Authority
playbooks.summary: Build content clusters around a primary entity, connecting related pages to boost topical authority and SEO performance.
playbooks.useWhen: A website lacks internal linking between related entity pages or aims to establish stronger topical authority.
playbooks.avoidWhen: The site already has sufficient internal linking or has no plans for entity-driven content strategy.
playbooks.expectedOutcomes: Improved topical authority, enhanced internal link equity, better SEO rankings for targeted topics.

antiPatterns.title: Keyword Stuffing in Heading Hierarchy
antiPatterns.description: Using exact-match keywords in H2/H3 headings to the point of sacrificing readability and topical coherence.
antiPatterns.signalsJson: ["heading density > 3% for single term","semantic field score < 0.4","high keyword repetition in consecutive headings"]

antiPatterns.title: Orphaned Entity Mentions
antiPatterns.description: Mentioning a brand, product, or person in content without linking to or cross-referencing a canonical entity page.
antiPatterns.signalsJson: ["entity mentioned without schema markup","no links to entity authority page","entity not in knowledge graph"]

antiPatterns.title: AI Answer Evasion
antiPatterns.description: Writing in a style that avoids direct declarative statements, making content unsuitable for AI summarization and citation.
antiPatterns.signalsJson: ["passive voice > 40%","no direct answer in first 60 words","hedged language in every paragraph"]

antiPatterns.title: Conflicting Brand Descriptions Across Owned Channels
antiPatterns.description: Publishing inconsistent brand descriptions across owned platforms undermines entity authority and can lead to reduced visibility in GEO marketing efforts.
antiPatterns.signalsJson: ["Brand descriptions vary across Wikipedia, LinkedIn, Wikidata, etc.","Knowledge panels show inconsistent information about the brand."]

antiPatterns.title: Orphaned Pages Diluting Entity Signals
antiPatterns.description: Failing to link pages with topical context leads to orphaned pages, weakening a website's entity signals and topical authority.
antiPatterns.signalsJson: ["Presence of pages with no internal links pointing to or from them","Low topical or semantic connection between pages within the same content cluster","Decreased performance in entity-based search metrics"]

examples.title: Answer-First Rewrite: SaaS Pricing Page
examples.description: Original opening: "Our pricing is designed to scale with your team..." — Rewritten: "Sieve costs $299/month for teams up to 10 users, with enterprise pricing available for larger organizations." The rewritten version received 3x more AI citation appearances within 30 days.

examples.title: Entity Knowledge Panel: B2B Software Company
examples.description: After implementing Organization schema and standardizing NAP across 12 directories, a mid-market SaaS achieved knowledge panel eligibility within 67 days. Key signal: Wikidata entry with sameAs links to official website and LinkedIn.

documents.title: Entity Building for B2B SaaS Guide
Modern SEO and GEO Principles Guide

recentTraces.query: Why is my blog not ranking on Google
Why is my blog not ranking on Google

recentRuns.rationale_summary: There are multiple reasons why a blog may not rank on Google, including thin or copied content, poor SEO implementation, lack of semantic HTML, inconsistent entity signals, or outdated material—but more specific details about the blog are needed to diagnose its ranking issues conclusively.
recentRuns.missing_data: A detailed audit of the blog's SEO practices, content quality, semantic HTML usage, and schema markup implementation is required to determine why it isn't ranking.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: There are multiple possible reasons your blog might not be ranking, many tied to content discoverability and originality as highlighted in the provided principles and rules.
recentRuns.missing_data: Specific audit details of your blog's current SEO practices and technical structure are missing.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: The system validates claims through consensus and structured content principles that align with transparent, authoritative sourcing, reducing reliance on single-source inputs.
recentRuns.missing_data: Improved transparency in how source chunks are cross-referenced and aggregated would close gaps.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: SEO success depends on establishing authority, optimizing for user and crawler needs, and ensuring strong technical infrastructure. Consistency and optimization are universally critical.
recentRuns.missing_data: Website-specific data and industry benchmarks would provide clearer insights into the most impactful action item.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: Establishing entity authority and ensuring structured data integration are critical for search engines to recognize and rank the site effectively, supported by optimized intent-aligned content and discoverable visuals.
recentRuns.missing_data: More case study and benchmark data would enhance the understanding of SEO technique efficacy.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: SEO principles are well-documented, covering technical optimization, content quality, and authority-building strategies. Synthesizing these with actionable rules provides a clear framework for improving visibility.
recentRuns.missing_data: Comparative impact metrics of specific SEO tactics are lacking.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: By addressing foundational SEO and entity authority gaps first, Default Brand can enhance its overall digital visibility while simultaneously preparing for future advances in generative and AI-driven search technologies.
recentRuns.missing_data: Clear audit of Default Brand's entity authority and content readiness is the most critical data gap.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: Starting with entity and topical authority while aligning for emerging generative search systems provides clarity, scalability, and competitive readiness for Default Brand.
recentRuns.missing_data: Specifically, we lack data on Default Brand's current entity-related inconsistencies across platforms, including NAP and knowledge panel gaps.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: Entity authority and unification are central for improving B2B SaaS visibility and minimizing traffic leakage, making [PB:2], [PB:3], and [PB:4] the strongest fits.
recentRuns.missing_data: Key gaps include the volume/structure of content and the current consistency of entity signals across platforms.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: Entity authority and unification playbooks are directly aligned with the brand's need to establish a strong authoritative presence online, while GEO-focused content optimization is of secondary priority unless a large relevant content corpus exists.
recentRuns.missing_data: Data on online presence and content maturity would refine recommendations.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: Entity authority hinges on consistency, structured data, and alignment with user and search engine needs, per multiple principles and rules provided. Comprehensive insights into best practices emerge but lack industry-specific clarity.
recentRuns.missing_data: Concrete case studies and measurable outcomes on entity authority would enhance specificity and actionable insights.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: The response is grounded in well-documented principles that highlight the shift from traditional keyword-based strategies to intent-driven and structured entity-based optimizations as essential for SEO success.
recentRuns.missing_data: Detailed, platform-specific examples or metrics for entity authority would enhance confidence.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: To prioritize SEO growth, Test Brand should focus first on improving content readiness for generative AI and building robust entity authority, provided the necessary conditions for applicability are met.
recentRuns.missing_data: Verification of content library size and entity presence is critical to precision.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: The proposed strategy sets foundational priorities: establishing entity authority, optimizing for generative content citation, and aligning content to user intent—all of which align with search engine evolution and Test Brand's competitive landscape.
recentRuns.missing_data: Detailed knowledge graph and content inventory metrics are the key missing elements.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: The recommended starting position focuses on fortifying Test Brand's entity authority for better traffic attribution and visibility, while optimizing existing content for AI citation capture and query intent alignment.
recentRuns.missing_data: Competitive intel on Competitor A's knowledge panel and AI citation advantages.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: The principles provide a clear framework for structuring GEO content but do not address engine-specific nuances or iterative performance data insights.
recentRuns.missing_data: Specific optimization metrics and engine-adjustment nuances are lacking.

recentRuns.rationale_summary: The principles provided offer clear guidelines for structuring content to align with the needs of generative engines, covering entity consistency, query intent, authoritative consensus, and structural optimization.
recentRuns.missing_data: Real-world examples and performance analytics would enhance the actionable specificity of this guidance.
